source: Meta Business Insights, Meta's published SMB advertiser performance data, WordStream 2025 Digital Advertising Benchmark Report, industry aggregated data from ad platforms (Q1 2025)

facebook_ads.median_ctr_note: Click-through rate varies by ad format (video typically higher, static image lower)
facebook_ads.advantage_plus_note: Advantage+ Shopping campaigns deliver 32% lower CPA than manual campaigns on average
best_objectives_smb: Traffic (for brand awareness and site visits)
facebook_ads.notes: SMB advertisers typically operate in the median range. Results vary significantly by industry, creative quality, and audience targeting precision. ROAS and CPA improve with Advantage+ and dynamic creative optimization.

instagram_advantages: Higher CTR than Facebook (2.41% vs 2.19%) due to visual-first feed
Younger demographic (18-44 skew) ideal for lifestyle, fashion, beauty brands
Shopping integration: Shoppable posts, product tags, Instagram Shop seamless to ads
Stories and Reels lower CPM but higher engagement for video-first creative
best_performing_content: Product-in-context (e.g., shoes being worn, not just on white background)
instagram_ads.notes: Instagram performs best for SMBs selling visual products or lifestyle services (beauty, fashion, home, fitness). Lower CPA and higher engagement than Facebook for these verticals.

outbound_conversation_categories.cost_model: Lower rate per message. Examples: order confirmation, appointment reminder, password reset.
Lowest rate. One-time use codes for account access.
Higher rate per message. Limited to within 24 hours of customer-initiated conversation or with template approval.

ideal_use_cases_smb: Appointment reminders and confirmations (low cost, high engagement)
Order status and shipping updates (builds trust, reduces support inquiries)
Follow-up offers and win-back campaigns (high response rates justify cost)
Lead nurturing for B2B services (doctors, dentists, accountants)
whatsapp_business_api.notes: WhatsApp's per-message cost is offset by 85% open rate and 30-50% response rate. Most profitable for repeat-use cases (appointment reminders, order updates) where cost is under $0.05 per conversation, not bulk marketing campaigns.

google_search_smb.advantage_vs_meta: Google Search has higher intent (person is actively searching) but higher CPC. Meta has lower CPC and better for brand awareness.
google_search_smb.when_to_use_vs_meta: Use Google Search for high-intent, immediate-need searches ('emergency plumber', 'buy Nike shoes online'). Use Meta for awareness, retargeting, and lower CPA.

advantages: Lowest CPM among major platforms (good for brand awareness)
Exceptional engagement (viral potential for trending content)
disadvantages: Lower conversion rate than Meta or Google for SMB products
Requires trend-aware, entertainment-first creative (harder for traditional SMBs)
Attribution less reliable for conversion tracking
tiktok_ads_smb.smb_fit: Best for DTC brands with visually engaging products and younger target audiences. Less suitable for B2B services or older demographic targets.

advantages: Great for B2B SMB services (consulting, accounting, law)
Strong targeting by job title, company size, industry
disadvantages: Most expensive platform (highest CPM and CPC)
Longest sales cycle (enterprise and B2B require nurturing)
Not suitable for B2C or consumer products
linkedin_ads_smb.smb_fit: Only use if targeting business decision-makers (e.g., marketing manager at a company wanting to buy services). Not for consumer brands.

advantages: Highest intent (person searching specifically for product)
disadvantages: Requires active inventory management and feed optimization
High competition in popular categories drives up CPC
google_shopping_smb.smb_fit: Essential for e-commerce SMBs. Use alongside Meta for awareness + retargeting strategy.

meta_internal_smb_benchmarks.advantage_plus_early_growth_percent: 15-20% ROAS improvement in first 30 days for SMBs switching from manual

typical_progression.week_1: Campaign launch, audience selection, creative optimization begins
typical_progression.week_2: 15-25 conversions accumulated, cost per result stabilizes
typical_progression.week_3_4: AI begins optimizing (if using Advantage+), ROAS trend emerges
typical_progression.month_2: SMB decides to maintain, increase, or pause based on first-month results

professional_services_consulting.seasonal_pattern: More consistent year-round; slight uptick in Q1 (budget planning)

disclaimers_and_notes.note_1: Benchmarks are aggregated from published Meta data, WordStream reports, and industry surveys. Individual SMB results vary based on industry, creative quality, audience targeting, product/service quality, and conversion tracking setup.
disclaimers_and_notes.note_2: ROAS and CPA assume proper conversion tracking. If conversion tracking is not set up correctly, results may appear worse than they are (pixel not firing, offline conversions not attributed, etc.).
disclaimers_and_notes.note_3: Advantage+ requires 50+ conversions in the first 30 days to fully train. SMBs with lower conversion volume may see better results with manual campaigns or lookalike audiences.
disclaimers_and_notes.note_4: Median benchmarks are more realistic for typical SMBs than average benchmarks, which can be skewed by outliers (high-performing e-commerce brands, well-resourced teams).
disclaimers_and_notes.note_5: Cost-per-result (ROAS, CPA) is heavily influenced by the SMB's product quality, landing page experience, customer service, and fulfillment. Advertising amplifies both good experiences and bad ones.
